test_completeness.justification: Trivial compliance-metadata edit: a single integer ceiling (current 620 -> 509) in shipwright_bloat_baseline.json. No app behavior is introduced or changed, so the Test Completeness Ledger is auto-n/a at trivial complexity per F5. The sole consumer of the edited file (the vendored anti-ratchet hook) is verified directly (worktree mode, status=ok).

surface_verification.justification: Compliance-baseline metadata edit consumed ONLY by the vendored Python anti-ratchet git hook + .github/workflows/bloat-check.yml. No webui runtime / web / API / store / WS / CLI surface appears in the diff (one integer in a *_baseline.json file). Verified directly: the anti-ratchet hook in worktree mode returns status=ok with zero ratchets (measured routes.ts = 509 <= new ceiling 509), and the full server unit suite (1637/1637) is green against the byte-identical base.

degraded.detail: The worktree has no node_modules (a JSON-metadata-only change touches zero TypeScript; no test imports shipwright_bloat_baseline.json). The full server unit suite (vitest run, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) was executed in the main tree at HEAD c773d3e -- byte-identical to this worktree except the single tightened integer -- and passed 1637/1637. The verification gate specific to this change (the anti-ratchet hook) was run inside the worktree.
